title: Min Stack
coreIdea: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element all in O(1) time. Achieved by maintaining a second stack that tracks the minimum value at each point alongside the main stack.
recognitionCues: asks to design a data structure with O(1) operations
getMin or similar constant-time min/max retrieval required alongside standard stack operations
approach: Maintain two stacks: the main stack for actual values, and a min-stack where each push also pushes the current minimum (min of new value and previous min-stack top). Pop removes from both stacks together, keeping them in sync.
